advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from safety surface, correctness-critical Power-of-Ten rules, and correctness-oriented structural checks.
advisorySubscores.title: Correctness

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from lifecycle/governance evidence, reviewability-oriented Power-of-Ten rules, and maintainability-heavy structural checks.
advisorySubscores.title: Maintainability

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from async/concurrency structural checks and bounded-control-flow review signals.
advisorySubscores.title: Concurrency / Async

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from runtime-allocation proxies, resource-lifecycle checks, and bounded-allocation / bounded-loop review rules.
advisorySubscores.title: Resource Discipline

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from verification signals, build/tooling complexity, and analyzability-oriented Power-of-Ten rules.
advisorySubscores.title: Verification / Reviewability

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from the full locked rubric as a broad readiness-oriented advisory synthesis.
advisorySubscores.title: Assurance / Provenance

guideline: Method: weighted checkpoint scoring across Safety (15%), Verification (15%), Build/Tooling (10%), Lifecycle/Governance (10%), NASA/JPL Power of Ten (25%), and Advanced Structural Checks (25%).
Checkpoint credit: pass/clear/applied = 1.0, indeterminate/partial = 0.5, elevated/not applied = 0.0.
Fairness rule: raw motif counts do not linearly reduce the score; each checkpoint contributes once so large crates are not punished simply for having more code.
Informational-only signals such as DSFB heuristic motif matches, hotspot counts, and capability flags like no_std/no_alloc are reported but excluded from the score denominator.
Interpretation: this is a broad improvement and review-readiness score for source-visible controls and evidence, not a certification and not a measure of runtime correctness.

conclusionLenses.certificationPreparation: For certification-oriented preparation, treat JPL-R9, CMD-BUF, TIME-WAIT, P10-3, P10-4 as pre-review cleanup targets and evidence-organizing prompts rather than certification outcomes.
conclusionLenses.complianceReadiness: 3 finding(s) directly affect analyzability, reproducibility, or review traceability. DSFB may support internal review against standards-oriented expectations, but it does not certify compliance.
conclusionLenses.distributedOperational: Operational pressure is most visible in CMD-BUF, P10-5, H-SERDE-01, H-ALLOC-01, H-CHAN-01. These findings are the most likely to matter later in runtime replay, backpressure review, or production-style load investigation.
conclusionLenses.rustMaintainer: Use the 58.8% overall score as a broad code-improvement target, not a compliance or certification badge. The highest-value maintainer work is concentrated in JPL-R9, TIME-WAIT, P10-3, P10-4, P10-5.

guidanceSemantics.nonCertificationStatement: DSFB does not certify compliance with IEC, ISO, RTCA, MIL, NIST, or other standards. Use this audit as a guideline for improvement and review readiness.

message.text: H-SERDE-01 matched 12 source motif hit(s) with reason code SerializationDrift.

properties.provenance: serde deserialization with growing payload; schema migration overhead
properties.remediation: Review payload growth, eager allocation, and schema-boundary handling on the serialization path.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Review the emitted evidence and add a targeted regression or replay check on the affected path.

message.text: H-CLOCK-01 matched 11 source motif hit(s) with reason code ClockDriftDivergence.

properties.provenance: TSC vs HPET clock source discrepancy between cluster nodes
properties.remediation: Prefer monotonic clocks for control logic and isolate wall-clock use to presentation or external protocol boundaries.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Add a regression test that isolates monotonic timing logic from wall-clock presentation or protocol boundaries.

snippet.text: let mut out = Vec::with_capacity((config.ticks as usize) * (config.agents as usize) * 2);

message.text: H-ALLOC-01 matched 3 source motif hit(s) with reason code MemoryPressureEscalation.

properties.provenance: Vec<T> capacity doubling in hot loop; jemalloc arena exhaustion
properties.remediation: Audit hot-loop allocation sites and prefer bounded or reserved growth on steady-state paths.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Benchmark the flagged path under steady load and inspect allocation counts before and after preallocation changes.

message.text: H-ERR-01 matched 3 source motif hit(s) with reason code SustainedLatencyDrift.

properties.provenance: Connection pool exhaustion; timeout cascade in microservice chain
properties.remediation: Audit retry policy, escalation path, and whether repeated failure surfaces are bounded and jittered.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Run a failure-path test and confirm retry pacing, cancellation, and escalation remain bounded.

message.text: H-CHAN-01 matched 1 source motif hit(s) with reason code ChannelBackpressureOnset.

properties.remediation: Check boundedness, receiver saturation handling, and whether producers can observe downstream backpressure.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Exercise a producer-faster-than-consumer test and confirm queue depth remains bounded and observable.

message.text: All loops have a fixed upper bound: 3 potentially unbounded `loop`/`while` construct(s) observed.

properties.remediation: Add explicit upper bounds, timeout guards, or fixed-step limits so loop behavior is reviewable.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Add a regression test that demonstrates a visible loop bound, timeout, or cancellation path on the flagged logic.

snippet.text: let mut out = Vec::with_capacity((config.ticks as usize) * (config.agents as usize) * 2);

message.text: No dynamic allocation after initialization: 111 heap-allocation motif(s) observed, including 111 runtime-core signal(s). This crate-level scan cannot distinguish initialization-only allocation from steady-state allocation.

properties.remediation: Move dynamic allocation to initialization paths or document and bound the steady-state allocation sites.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Profile the flagged path under steady-state load and confirm no avoidable heap growth remains after initialization.

message.text: Functions stay within a single-sheet size budget (~60 LOC): 7 function(s) exceed the 60-line threshold.

properties.remediation: Split large functions into reviewable units with clearer local invariants and narrower responsibilities.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Split the function and add narrower tests that name the local invariants introduced by the refactor.

snippet.text: function `ingest_stream` has 0 assertion site(s) across 154 lines
function `speed_over_ground` has 0 assertion site(s) across 106 lines
function `proximity_pairs` has 0 assertion site(s) across 104 lines
function `append` has 0 assertion site(s) across 70 lines

message.text: Assertion density averages at least two per function: Estimated assertion density is 0.08 per function across 53 extracted function(s).

properties.remediation: Replace catch-all control flow with explicit state handling or document the fallback state as intentional.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Add state-transition tests that cover the previously catch-all path explicitly.

message.text: Return values are checked and parameters are validated: 0 explicit discard site(s) and 15 unwrap/expect site(s) observed. Parameter validation cannot be proven by this scan.

properties.remediation: Propagate errors explicitly rather than unwrapping, or document the invariant that justifies the unwrap/expect.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Replace unwrap/expect with explicit handling or add an invariant test that proves the extraction precondition.

message.text: Pedantic warnings and static analyzers are enforced: Observed warning/analyzer signal(s), but the full Power-of-Ten requirement for pedantic warnings plus regular analyzer use is not established. Warning signals: 0, analyzer signals: 1.

properties.remediation: Keep warnings and analyzer gates active in CI so the audit surface stays reviewable over time.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Keep analyzer and warnings-as-errors gates in CI and record the expected toolchain surface in the repo docs.

message.text: Unchecked extraction / dereference safety audit: 15 unwrap/expect-like site(s) observed; these deserve explicit invariant review in high-assurance code.

properties.remediation: Review the finding against the emitted evidence and either tighten the local structure or document the local invariant.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Use the evidence block to write the smallest targeted regression or review note that proves the intended invariant.

message.text: Hard-coded timing assumption audit: 1 hard-coded wait motif(s) observed. Review whether these are deterministic control waits or deadline-free timing assumptions.

properties.remediation: Review the finding against the emitted evidence and either tighten the local structure or document the local invariant.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Use the evidence block to write the smallest targeted regression or review note that proves the intended invariant.

message.text: Hazardous command buffering audit: 2 command/control queue motif(s) observed without TTL/staleness/sequence guard signals.

properties.remediation: Add TTL, sequence, staleness, or cancellation guards so queued control messages cannot accumulate invisibly.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Add queue tests that demonstrate staleness, TTL, cancellation, or sequence handling under backlog.
